parts.text:
Your task is to execute the scheduled task below as specified in that task's description.

If the scheduled work is complete and nothing needs reporting, stop taking actions. Do not use cron to mark the run complete, re-run it, or modify schedules. A later terminal phase will handle completion for you.

A normal assistant message ends this execution pass. Do not end the execution pass with a progress update, a promise to wait, or a statement like "I'll wait for the subagents." The later terminal phase cannot do task work, write files, call ordinary tools, or repair incomplete output; it can only choose the final cron handoff tool.

If you spawn subagents or start asynchronous work, keep working until their results are available in this execution pass and you have completed any parent-owned synthesis, verification, persistence, and artifact writes required by the scheduled task. If you cannot consume the child results before your assistant response would complete the execution pass, do not delegate the required work that way; do the required work directly in this worker turn instead.

[WORKER TASK]
job_id: dreaming
mode: task

[BODY]
# Dreaming Trigger

This scheduler worker is only a trigger. Do not perform Dreaming work here.

1. Read `run_id` from `JARVIS_TRACE_CONTEXT`.
2. Call the daemon product endpoint and wait for it to finish:

```sh
sandbox_api_sock="${JARVIS_SANDBOX_API_SOCK:-/run/hatch/sandbox-api/api.sock}"
run_id="$(python3 - <<'PY'
import json, os, sys

trace = json.loads(os.environ.get("JARVIS_TRACE_CONTEXT") or "{}")
run_id = str(trace.get("run_id") or "").strip()
if not run_id:
    sys.exit("missing run_id in JARVIS_TRACE_CONTEXT")
print(run_id)
PY
)"
curl --fail-with-body --silent --show-error \
  --unix-socket "$sandbox_api_sock" \
  --request POST \
  --header 'content-type: application/json' \
  --header "x-hatch-worker-trace-context: ${JARVIS_TRACE_CONTEXT:-}" \
  --data "{\"run_id\":\"${run_id}\"}" \
  http://hatch/dreaming
```

If the endpoint succeeds, stop after the endpoint call. The endpoint JSON printed
by `curl` is the product result for the outer scheduler resolution phase. Do not
call scheduler terminal tools from this trigger body. If the endpoint fails, stop
immediately after the failed endpoint call; the daemon endpoint aborts this
trigger worker so the scheduler records the failed run through its normal failure
path.

## Scheduler resolution criteria

In the later scheduler resolution phase, inspect the endpoint JSON printed by
`curl`.

Call `nothing_to_report({})` for every successful Dreaming endpoint result.
Dreaming never reports directly to the main agent from scheduler resolution.

[RUN CONTEXT]
scheduled_for_local: Sun 2026-06-14 22:18:13 UTC (UTC)
trigger_reason: scheduled
attempt: 1

parts.text:
You are a cron worker, not the main agent. Any assistant text you produced during execution is internal scratch text and has not been delivered to the user.

Your execution pass for this scheduled task run is complete.

Your task is to call exactly one final tool.

Before choosing, determine whether this run qualifies for silent completion.

`nothing_to_report` and `notify_main_agent` are top-level tools in this resolution context, so call them by name. `space_action` only routes to Space-exported actions, so wrapping a terminal call through it would look for a Space action that doesn't exist.

Call `nothing_to_report({})` only if all of the following are true:
- There is no substantive result, alert, update, requested delivery, or execution-health issue to hand back to the main agent.
- The run had sufficient, fresh evidence for the task's requested time horizon.
- Every required dependency (API, data source, file, credential, tool, artifact, etc.) was fully available, current, and not replaced by a lower-quality workaround. There were no failures, gaps, stale inputs, partial checks, or inconsistent evidence.
- Silence accurately represents a clean, uneventful, fully successful run.

If not, or if you are unsure, call `notify_main_agent(message)` with a concise explanation. This is only an internal handoff; the main agent decides whether anything should surface externally.

These tools are terminal. Call exactly one of them, only once, and only now at the end of the run.
Do not continue executing the task. Do not call any other tool. Do not write a normal assistant reply instead of choosing one of the tools.
The main agent owns any later delivery and dedupe decision, including any external channel delivery.
